evals.prompt: I want to start using JAIBA on this repo. It's a fresh Python service, nothing set up yet. Can you scaffold it?
evals.expected_output: Detects no vendor agent dir -> uses .agents/skills as install target. Creates .ai/ skeleton (memory/ specs/ session/ vendored/) plus .ai/.gitignore ignoring session/ and tools-state.md. Installs AGENTS.md at root. Installs the project-scoped skill set. Runs the tool check writing .ai/tools-state.md. Hands off to update-brain:initialize. Ends with a closing report.

Repo already has its own AGENTS.md at root. Skill must NOT overwrite it -- it stops and offers the two non-destructive options (back up + replace, or install as AGENTS.jaiba.md to coexist) before continuing the rest of the bootstrap.

evals.prompt: can you scaffold jaiba into this project? I think someone may have started already
evals.expected_output: Repo already has a populated .ai/memory/constitution.md (real content, not bare template). Skill recognizes the project is already JAIBA-instrumented, refuses to re-scaffold/clobber, and routes the developer to update-brain (and/or planning/ask) instead.

evals.name: all-skillset-skills-already-global
evals.prompt: Scaffold jaiba into this repo. It's a fresh Go service.

context.global_skills_state: every skill listed in skills/scaffold/assets/skillset.txt (planning, specification, ask, fast, update-brain, create-knowledge, jaiba-doctor, caveman) is already present in `npx skills list -g` output

evals.expected_output: Before installing anything, ran `npx skills list -g` and found every skillset.txt entry already global. Installed nothing -- not globally, not project-locally. Told the developer their global skillset already covers this project and pointed them at `npx skills update -g` to stay current. Continued the rest of the bootstrap (.ai/ skeleton, AGENTS.md, tool-check pointed at the global skills directory, hand-off to update-brain:initialize). Closing report explicitly notes Case A (already global, nothing installed).

evals.name: missing-skills-offered-global-or-local
evals.prompt: Set up jaiba here, this is a new TypeScript monorepo.

context.global_skills_state: `npx skills list -g` returns only jaiba-scaffold -- none of the skillset.txt entries (planning, specification, ask, fast, update-brain, create-knowledge, jaiba-doctor, caveman) are global yet

evals.expected_output: Ran `npx skills list -g`, found none of the skillset.txt entries present, and asked the developer ONCE -- before installing anything -- whether the missing skills should be installed globally (`npx skills add -y <source> --skill <skill> -g`) or project-locally (same command without `-g`). Did not install anything before getting an answer. After the developer answers, installs only the missing entries with the chosen scope, then continues the rest of the bootstrap (.ai/ skeleton, AGENTS.md, tool-check, hand-off to update-brain:initialize).

evals.name: mixed-global-and-local-after-choice
evals.prompt: Yes, install the missing ones locally for this project.

context.prior_turn: scaffold already ran `npx skills list -g` and found planning, specification, and ask already global, but fast, update-brain, create-knowledge, jaiba-doctor, and caveman missing -- and just asked the developer global vs. local for the missing ones
context.repo_state: .ai/ skeleton and AGENTS.md not yet created

evals.expected_output: Installed only the previously-missing skills (fast, update-brain, create-knowledge, jaiba-doctor, caveman) project-locally (no -g flag), one by one. Did not reinstall or duplicate planning, specification, or ask, which were already global. Continued the bootstrap: .ai/ skeleton, AGENTS.md, then ran the tool check against BOTH the project's local skills dir and the global skills dir (colon-separated paths to check-tools.sh), then handed off to update-brain:initialize. Closing report distinguishes which skills came from where.
